resources.title: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
resources.description: Free, confidential support 24/7 for anyone in emotional distress. Call or text 988.

resources.title: Crisis Text Line
resources.description: Text HOME to 741741 to reach a trained crisis counselor any time, day or night.

resources.title: Eldercare Locator
resources.description: A public service connecting older adults and caregivers to local services: meals, transportation, home care, and respite.

resources.title: Family Caregiver Alliance
resources.description: Fact sheets, a services-by-state navigator, and support for families managing long-term care at home.

resources.title: GriefShare
resources.description: Faith-based grief recovery support groups that meet locally and online, with a daily encouragement email.

resources.title: The Dougy Center
resources.description: Guidance and peer support for grieving children, teens, and their families, with free downloadable resources.

resources.title: National Hospice and Palliative Care Organization — CaringInfo
resources.description: Plain-language guides on advance directives, choosing hospice, and serious-illness decisions, including free state forms.

resources.title: Respite Care Locator (ARCH National Respite Network)
resources.description: Find short-term, temporary relief so caregivers can rest, run errands, or simply breathe.

resources.title: What's Your Grief
resources.description: Practical, compassionate articles and worksheets on coping with loss, written by two mental-health professionals.

resources.title: Caregiver Action Network — Family Caregiver Toolbox
resources.description: Checklists and a peer help desk for everyday caregiving questions, from medication tracking to talking with doctors.

resources.title: National Academy of Elder Law Attorneys (NAELA)
resources.description: Find an attorney for wills, powers of attorney, guardianship, and planning for long-term care costs.

resources.title: BenefitsCheckUp (National Council on Aging)
resources.description: Confidential screening that finds programs to help pay for medications, food, utilities, and care.

resources.title: Hospice Foundation of America
resources.description: Webinars, podcasts, and grief resources to help families understand and make the most of hospice care.

resources.title: Sesame Workshop — Grief resources for young children
resources.description: Gentle videos, storybooks, and printable activities to help young children name and move through big feelings.

resources.title: Modern Loss
resources.description: Candid stories and a warm community for the long, messy, unscripted reality of life after loss.

resources.title: Well Spouse Association
resources.description: Support specifically for spousal caregivers, including peer forums and local support groups.

resources.title: PREPARE for Your Care
resources.description: An easy, step-by-step program to think through medical wishes and document them with free advance-directive forms.
